Merge pull request #455 from tiiuae/mess_bridge_hotfix2

Mess bridge hotfix2

commit_title: Traffic multiplication appears to be due to a regression in the Lower/Upper Mess Bridge's (lmb/umb) even though they appear to be correctly configured in terms of forwarding database (bridge fdb) and port isolation (ip -d link show) so here is a bruteforce hotfix just dropping any traffic that should not be happening anyway.
commit_message:
Traffic multiplication appears to be due to a regression in the Lower/Upper Mess Bridge's (lmb/umb) even though they appear to be correctly configured in terms of forwarding database (bridge fdb) and port isolation (ip -d link show) so here is a bruteforce hotfix just dropping any traffic that should not be happening anyway.

WARNING: untested with more than 2 devices (which is required to be able to reproduced the issue) as I do not have access to more than 2 right now, but FWIW this hotfix does not seem to break anything at least between 2 devices.

Add settings/command Unittest

Jira-Id: SECO-4967, SECO-4961

Signed-off-by: Mika Joenpera <mika.joenpera@unikie.com>

Merge pull request #453 from tiiuae/force_router_advertisements_leak

commit_title: Hardcoding br-lan in radvd configuration to force Router Advertisements to
commit_message:
Hardcoding br-lan in radvd configuration to force Router Advertisements to
leak to the whole mesh i.e. make all external devices self-configure with
all the SLAAC prefixes, for example in a Laptop1 - CSL1 - CSL2 - Laptop2
scenario, Laptop1 and Laptop2 would both SLAAC with the prefixes from both
CSL1 and CSL2.

Improve CBMA Adaptation error handling

Lower/Upper CBMA setup failure handling is improved so
that both lower and upper CBMA controller is stopped in
case of any failure in order to remove any intermediate
CBMA internal interfaces so that they are not counted as
black interfaces during next setup trial.

commit_title: Merging bug fixes and improvisation for MS 1.5 from mesh_com_1.5_demo branch
commit_message:
Merging bug fixes and improvisation for MS 1.5 from mesh_com_1.5_demo branch

* main.py --> drop duplicates in sectable based on IP column,  added condition to run decision engine and quarantine only if global table is not empty, set quarantine time to SEC_BEAT_TIME, set initial exchange server socket timeout to 0.75*SEC_BEAT_TIME
* main_with_menu.py --> In extable(), set initial exchange server socket timeout to 0.75*SEC_BEAT_TIME
* mutual.py --> Replaced hardcoded connection exceptions with general exception and increased sleep time in case of exception to avoid termination of mutual authentication
* ness_main.py --> changed malicious condition to count_malicious > len(num_servers) / 2 instead of count_malicious >= len(num_servers) / 2 and changed result in case count_malicious == len(num_servers) to suspicious [194]
* continuous/utils.py --> In launchCA, all client threads parallelly. In update_table_ca, added mac of neighbor by running batctl t ip instead of keeping '-----'
* continuous/client.py --> Set client socket timeout to 10 seconds
* * continuous/server.py --> Changes to run client threads parallelly, set server socket timeout to 15 seconds, period = 1, total no of auths in 1 round to 3
* utils/utils.py --> Inside exchage_table(), set initial send_flag to 0 if 'To_Send' is empty for all exchange_table entries, else 1 (instead of using 1), set an initial socket timeout of 0.75*SEC_BEAT_TIME so that the while loop breaks if nothing is received (no neighbors), set socket timeout inside the while loop to 15 seconds
* quarantine.py --> add conditions to drop forward and output packets from/ to blocked node

Jira_Id : MSS20-136

Signed-off-by: Selina Shrestha <selina.shrestha@tii.ae>

commit_title: Changes for mcc and bridge compatibility with MS 1.5, minor fix to get mesh interface
commit_message:
Changes for mcc and bridge compatibility with MS 1.5, minor fix to get mesh interface

* bridge_settings.sh --> Script to setup bridge
* mcc_settings.sh --> Script to setup mcc mode
* entrypoint.sh --> Removed bridge_settings(). For MS 1.0: if mcc mode run mcc_settings.sh, else run bridge_settings.sh after mesh_service. For MS 1.5: update mesh_com_11s.conf according to the mode selected.
* mesh_com_11s.conf --> set default bridge to false and meshint to bat0. Added concurrency and mcc_channel for mcc mode
* ConnectionMgr.py --> (For MS 1.5) Read concurrency, mcc_channel, bridge from mesh_com_11s.conf. Write mcc_channel to /opt/mesh.conf. Added clause to join thread that starts gateway service. After establishing mesh, if concurrency is set for mcc mode, run mcc_settings.sh, else if bridge is true, run bridge_settings.sh.
* mutual.py --> remove sleep after starting_mesh(). Added join thread in starting_mesh() instead
* header.py --> used correct function to get mesh interface in MESHINT = mesh_utils.get_mesh_interface_from_file()
* continuous/utils.py --> used correct function to get mesh interface in MESHINT = mesh_utils.get_mesh_interface_from_file()
* continuous/ca_main.py --> used correct function to get mesh interface in MESHINT = mesh_utils.get_mesh_interface_from_file()
* quarantine.py --> used correct function to get mesh interface in self.interface = mesh_utils.get_mesh_interface(mesh_utils.get_mesh_interface_from_file())

Jira_Id : MSS20-136

Signed-off-by: Selina Shrestha <selina.shrestha@tii.ae>

commit_title: Refactoring network setup process and scc/mcc support added
commit_message:
Refactoring network setup process and scc/mcc support added

Entrypoint refactored and all network related configuration was moved
to mesh-11s.sh (+mesh-helper.sh).  Simplified startup to support later
containerization for separate processes.

mesh-11s.sh was refactored also.  CommandLine parameters were removed and
settings are now directly read from used mesh configuration file.

Command interfaces supports also now ap+mesh_scc and ap+mesh_mcc commands.
UnitTests/Scripts updated accordingly.

Multipurpose Mesh test case added

test_mesh_connection.sh:
- can be used for NONE/SAE and 2.4GHz/5GHz
- can be used Mesh PtP, single hop and beyond
- Network topology needs to be handled by tester (no VLAN in use)

Example:
- DUT responsible for running test:
sudo ./test_mesh_connectio.sh -i 192.168.1.y -e NONE -f 2412 -m client -s 192.168.1.x

- DUTs respnsible to be part of the network(node):
sudo ./test_mesh_connectio.sh -i 192.168.1.x -e NONE -f 2412 -m server

  -i ipaddress    IP address to be used for node
  -e encryption   SAE or NONE
  -f frequency    Wi-Fi frequency in MHz
  -m mode         server or client.
                  server = test node which provides iperf3
                  client = test node which runs iperf3/ping tests against server
  -s address      server address to be used for test connection.

Merge branch 'xlsx_to_urdf' into 'cobalt-devel'

Kinematics xlsx to URDF

A new script is added to help the conversion from xlsx to URDF.

It uses a single sheet from the xlsx file to generate the link and joint blocks; later they can be adapted inside xacro files, using some macros, but they are easier to insert and process with vim.

This could also be a basic implementation for a better converter, but I don't see it right now.

Tested with this spreadsheet:
https://docs.google.com/a/pal-robotics.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0Al5pObG8WxjBdGhRSWxvV2NkOEthaU1wdnk4SGxxclE#gid=5

git-svn-id: svn+ssh://server/srv/svn/repos/trunk/pal-ros-pkg/catkin_pkgs/ant_robot@57684 5e370ff8-3418-0410-babe-3378cc20a00d

pal_ros_control upgrade

pal_ros_control:
- Allow a robot to provide read/write access to position, velocity and effort,
  plus write access to actuator current limit (up to seven values).

- The above are all optional, so one can choose what a robot has. Before,
  position read/write was enabled and hardcoded.

- Rework mechanism for specifying actuator port names, which became a bit
  cumbersome now that there are up to six values to spepcify (R/W pos, vel, eff)

- Add a hardware interface for actuator current limits.

<robot>_hardware:
- Adapt to the new actuator port names spacification.
- REEM, REEMC and ULNA expose an actuator current limiting interface.

Xmas update is here!

- Maps:
  - fixed a bug for which you could not use resizable rectangle maps.
  - Improved logging on send_* nodes
  - Improved nomenclature on models spawned in Gazebo
- Gazebo: models regarding world have been moved to `shelfino_gazebo`
- Projects:
  - Evacuation now uses timers to slowly populate the environment, to be
    changed in the future
- Navigation:
  - Removed two Nav2 nodes that were greatly slowing down the whole
    system. It should no be possible to spawn more robots without
    problems.
- BIG UPDATE: Santa Claus brought a Xmas hat to each Shelfino!
  Unfortunatelly the robots lose things easily, so they will probably
  lose these hats in the next couple weeks too.

change bootstrap script to use ansible on vagrant

Major changes to the previous bootstrap:
* ansible > 2.10 is required to run the script
* installation as non-root user under ~/SOP-Robot
  * moving also ros_control to ~/ros_control_ws
* creation of a non-interactive bash for ansible
* username defaults now to ansible_env.USER variable and no longer to
  vagrant
* installation of required python packages into a virtualenv in
  ~/SOP-Robot
* bootstrap script is now stateless

Minor changes:
* update launch settings from robot to reflect latest master
* change release in model.zip to have relative path https://github.com/ouspg/SOP-Robot/releases/tag/model
